title: Make product status-toggle idempotent on retry
prompt: Toggling a product between active and archived double-fires when the client retries, emitting two status-change events. Make the status change idempotent — a retried toggle to the same target status is a no-op that returns { ok: true, data }, not a second event. The product must stay fully reversible: active -> archived -> active -> archived must end archived.
acceptanceCriteria: a retried toggle to the same target returns { ok: true, data } without emitting a second event
the idempotency key is derived from request identity (a caller-supplied correlationId), not from mutable content
the aggregate stays reversible: active -> archived -> active -> archived ends 'archived', every flip persisted
a reversibility regression test exists and is proven to FAIL on a content-derived key

attempt.summary: Made the toggle idempotent by keying the domain event's idempotency key on product:${id}:status:${newStatus} — the target status. The double-fire is gone and determinism looks satisfied, but the key is content-derived: on a reversible aggregate the third flip (active -> archived -> active -> archived) reuses the second flip's key, collides on the (stream_key, idempotency_key) unique index, rolls back, and returns { ok: true } from input while the product stays 'active'. The lost write is silent — no test covers a re-flip, so it passes.
conventionViolations: derived the idempotency key from mutable content (target status) instead of request identity — collides two distinct intents on a reversible aggregate and silently drops the recovered write; see LSN-012
